def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta

    short_window = params['short_window']
    long_window = params['long_window']
    rsi_window = params['rsi_window']
    rsi_threshold = params['rsi_threshold']

    short_ma = df['close'].rolling(window=short_window).mean()
    long_ma = df['close'].rolling(window=long_window).mean()
    rsi = ta.momentum.rsi(df['close'], window=rsi_window)

    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    signals[(short_ma > long_ma) & (rsi > rsi_threshold)] = 1
    signals[(short_ma < long_ma) & (rsi < (100 - rsi_threshold))] = -1

    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: USD/JPY exhibits momentum-driven price movements when short-term moving averages cross long-term averages and RSI confirms overbought/oversold conditions.
